title: Sweet and Sour Power Hour
story: March 29th is National Lemon Chiffon Cake Day, when bakers everywhere whip up this fluffy, tangy treat! Lemon chiffon cake is super light and airy because it's made with beaten egg whites that create tiny bubbles. On this day, bakeries feature special lemon chiffon displays, families share their secret recipes, and social media fills with photos of perfectly golden slices. The cake tastes like sunshine with its bright lemon flavor and cloud-like texture that practically melts in your mouth!

title: Smoke and Mirrors Day: When Magic Meets Truth!
story: March 29th is Smoke and Mirrors Day, when people celebrate the art of illusion and question what's really true! Magicians perform extra tricks today, showing off classic "smoke and mirrors" effects that fool our eyes. But it's not just about magic shows - people also use this day to talk about being honest and spotting when someone might be trying to trick them with fancy words or confusing facts. Schools sometimes hold magic shows or teach kids how to think critically about information they see online.

title: When the Famous Falls Took a Break!
story: On March 29th, people celebrate one of the weirdest natural events ever recorded - the day Niagara Falls completely stopped flowing! Back in 1848, ice blocked the Niagara River so badly that the thundering waterfalls went silent for almost 30 hours. Today on March 29th, visitors to Niagara Falls share photos and stories about this incredible moment when you could actually walk on the dry riverbed where millions of gallons usually crash down every minute. Some people even joke about what they'd do if they could explore behind the falls without getting soaked!
